summary: Do not ship yet. P-2 remains unresolved because the 780s bound excludes an already-running legacy Job. No separate new critical issue or materially simpler complete approach was found.

findings.title: P-2 remains unresolved: a legacy Forbid Job escapes the bound
findings.body: `activeDeadlineSeconds` is added only to `CronJob.spec.jobTemplate`; Kubernetes does not retrofit template changes onto existing Jobs. If a pre-change Job is active during the single landing, it retains the old unbounded commands, while `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` skips every new Job carrying the deadline. The manifest-only preflight and synthetic L7 still pass, but production can remain heartbeat-free past `for: 15m` and emit the rollout-only page P-2 was meant to prevent.
findings.recommendation: Specify a declarative transition that handles an already-active legacy Job, such as a tested `Replace` policy, versioned CronJob rollover, or automated cleanup before rule activation. Make preflight lock that transition and add a rollout test beginning with an active pre-change Job.

next_steps: Revise the existing-Job transition contract and L7/preflight coverage.
